unsupportedGaps: Webhook side effects need sandbox provider keys before execution.

test_cards.title: Authenticated user can complete the primary workflow without hidden console failures
test_cards.goal: Prove the app's highest-value workflow works from first page load through final confirmation.
steps: Reuse captured local auth state.
Open dashboard from a clean browser context.
Create a new record with valid boundary data.
Verify confirmation, persisted state, and reload behavior.
test_cards.acceptanceCriteria: The record exists after reload and no console error or failed API request appears during the flow.

test_cards.title: Role boundary prevents client user from reaching admin-only repair actions
test_cards.goal: Confirm role separation is enforced at navigation, page load, and API mutation layers.
steps: Load captured client-role auth state.
Attempt direct navigation to admin repair task URL.
Attempt the backing mutation request from the browser context.
Capture server response and visible UI state.
test_cards.acceptanceCriteria: Client role receives a blocked state and cannot mutate or view admin-only repair tasks.

test_cards.title: Slow API response preserves form input and shows a recoverable state
test_cards.goal: Expose the common launch bug where slow services erase user work or double-submit forms.
steps: Throttle submit endpoint to 4 seconds.
Submit a valid form.
Verify disabled state, spinner, and no duplicate request.
Refresh after completion and confirm saved data.
test_cards.acceptanceCriteria: Only one mutation fires, user input remains visible, and the saved state survives reload.

test_cards.title: Mobile audit report is readable without horizontal overflow
test_cards.goal: Verify the client-ready report can be reviewed on mobile without clipped tables or overlapping action buttons.
steps: Open report page at 390px width.
Scroll every section.
Inspect issue table, evidence gallery, and repair task cards.
Capture screenshot and overflow metrics.
dataNeeds: Generated audit report with at least two findings
test_cards.acceptanceCriteria: No primary content overflows the viewport and all action labels remain readable.

test_cards.title: Webhook/payment side effects are declared instead of silently skipped
test_cards.goal: Prevent fake confidence by forcing unsupported third-party checks into the audit report.
steps: Inspect env and integration code references.
Detect missing sandbox keys.
Mark affected checks as blocked with exact reason.
dataNeeds: Sandbox provider keys or explicit skip approval
test_cards.acceptanceCriteria: The report names the blocked integration checks and does not count them as passed.

test_cards.title: No cross-user object access by swapping an id (IDOR)
test_cards.goal: A normal user requesting another owner's object id must be denied, never served the other owner's record.
steps: Capture a normal 'user' session.
GET another owner's object id (e.g. /api/orders/1).
Confirm the response is 401/403/404, not the other owner's data.
test_cards.acceptanceCriteria: As 'user', the swapped-id request returns 401/403/404, not a 2xx with another owner's object. WSTG-ATHZ-04 / CWE-639.

test_cards.title: Account detail endpoint enforces ownership
test_cards.goal: Prove the per-user record endpoint checks ownership server-side.
steps: As 'user', request the user's own object (allowed).
As 'user', request a neighbouring owner's id (denied).
test_cards.acceptanceCriteria: Owner can read their object; cross-owner read is blocked. WSTG-ATHZ-04 / CWE-639.

test_cards.title: Normal user is denied a privileged mutation (BFLA)
test_cards.goal: A privileged state-changing call by a normal user must be rejected with 401/403 before any write — the denial proves no state change.
steps: As 'user', POST the privileged endpoint (e.g. /api/admin/delete-user).
Confirm a 401/403, not a 2xx.
test_cards.acceptanceCriteria: As 'user', the privileged mutation returns 401/403 before any write. OWASP API5 / CWE-285.

test_cards.title: Update endpoint ignores privileged fields (mass-assignment)
test_cards.goal: Sending role:"admin"/isAdmin:true to a profile-update endpoint must be ignored, not persisted.
steps: As 'user', PATCH the profile with extra privileged fields.
Re-read the record and confirm role/isAdmin did not change.
test_cards.acceptanceCriteria: Privileged fields are not accepted or echoed as persisted. OWASP API3 / CWE-915.

test_cards.title: Session cookie carries HttpOnly, Secure, and SameSite
test_cards.goal: A session cookie without HttpOnly is JS-readable (XSS theft); without Secure it leaks over http; without SameSite it is CSRF-exposed.
steps: Log in and capture the Set-Cookie.
Check for HttpOnly, Secure, and SameSite attributes.
dataNeeds: A login that issues a session cookie
test_cards.acceptanceCriteria: The session Set-Cookie includes HttpOnly, Secure, and SameSite. CWE-1004 / CWE-614.

test_cards.title: CORS does not reflect a hostile Origin with credentials
test_cards.goal: An arbitrary Origin must not be echoed in Access-Control-Allow-Origin together with Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true.
steps: Send a request with Origin: https://evil.example.
Confirm the probe Origin is not reflected with credentials enabled.
test_cards.acceptanceCriteria: No credentialed reflection of an arbitrary Origin. CWE-942.

test_cards.title: HSTS (Strict-Transport-Security) is present
test_cards.goal: Without HSTS a browser will still try http first and can be downgraded before the redirect.
steps: GET / over https.
Confirm a Strict-Transport-Security response header.
test_cards.acceptanceCriteria: Response carries Strict-Transport-Security. OWASP Secure Headers / Mozilla TLS.

test_cards.title: http redirects to https
test_cards.goal: Plain http must redirect to https so credentials/cookies never travel in cleartext.
steps: GET the http:// origin.
Confirm a 301/308 whose Location is https.
dataNeeds: A reachable http endpoint to probe

test_cards.title: SQL-injection canary is handled safely
test_cards.goal: A non-destructive SQLi canary must not 500 the server or leak a database error.
steps: POST a field with the canary ' OR '1'='1.
Confirm no 500 and no SQL/engine error text in the body.
test_cards.acceptanceCriteria: No 500 and no DB-error text on the canary. WSTG-INPV / CWE-89.

test_cards.title: XSS canary is escaped, not reflected
test_cards.goal: A reflected XSS canary must come back escaped, never as live markup.
steps: Send the canary <svg/onload=alert(1)>.
Confirm the raw payload is not reflected unescaped.
test_cards.acceptanceCriteria: The raw XSS payload is not present unescaped in the response. WSTG-INPV / CWE-79.

test_cards.title: Security headers are present and carry safe values
test_cards.goal: The hardening headers that stop clickjacking, MIME-sniffing, and stack-banner leaks must be set.
steps: GET /.
Check Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy.
Confirm no X-Powered-By banner.
test_cards.acceptanceCriteria: CSP, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, and Referrer-Policy are present with safe values. OWASP Secure Headers.

test_cards.title: Secret and VCS files are not publicly downloadable
test_cards.goal: Config and version-control files must never be served to the public.
steps: Request /.env, /.env.local, /.git/config, /.git/HEAD.
Confirm each is blocked (not 200 with file content).
test_cards.acceptanceCriteria: No /.env* or /.git/* path returns downloadable content. OWASP WSTG configuration testing.

findings.title: Client role can load admin repair task detail by direct URL
findings.summary: Navigation hides the admin action, but the route-level loader returns repair task details for a client-role session.

findings.title: Mobile report table overflows 390px viewport
findings.summary: The evidence table uses fixed columns and pushes primary actions outside the viewport.

findings.title: Webhook checks blocked by missing sandbox keys
findings.summary: Payment/webhook confidence cannot be claimed until sandbox credentials are present or the check is explicitly waived.

repair_tasks.title: Enforce server-side role guard on repair task detail route
repair_tasks.why_it_matters: The UI hides admin actions, but direct navigation still exposes admin-only repair task details to a client user.
reproduction_steps: Login with captured client-role browser state.
Observe repair task title, likely files, and agent prompt in the rendered page.
repair_tasks.expected_behavior: Client role should receive a 403 page or redirect and API detail route should return 403.
repair_tasks.agent_prompt: Patch the repair task detail page and API route so authorization is enforced server-side. Keep admin access unchanged, return 403 for client users, and add a Playwright regression for direct URL access.

repair_tasks.title: Make audit report evidence table responsive
repair_tasks.why_it_matters: The launch audit is supposed to be client-ready, but the mobile report currently clips evidence and action labels.
reproduction_steps: Observe horizontal overflow and clipped action column.
repair_tasks.expected_behavior: Evidence rows collapse into readable stacked rows on narrow screens with no body overflow.
repair_tasks.agent_prompt: Refactor the audit evidence table responsive layout so mobile uses stacked row cards while desktop preserves the dense table. Verify no horizontal body overflow at 390px.

runner_tools.name: qa.inspect_repo
runner_tools.description: Inspect package metadata, scripts, route files, API handlers, env expectations, and existing test tooling.
runner_tools.outputShape: { framework, package_manager, scripts, route_count, api_route_count, env_keys }

runner_tools.name: qa.detect_app
runner_tools.description: Detect app stack and support tier so the audit report names first-class and generic coverage honestly.

runner_tools.name: qa.capture_auth_state
runner_tools.description: Open a controlled browser session and store encrypted local Playwright storage state without sending credentials to the web app.

runner_tools.name: qa.start_or_verify_app
runner_tools.description: Run or verify the local app URL, then collect console, network, and route reachability facts.

runner_tools.name: qa.run_test_card
runner_tools.description: Execute an approved test card with Playwright and persist trace, screenshot, video, and structured assertions.
runner_tools.inputShape: { campaign_id: string, test_card_id: string, storage_state_ref?: string }

runner_tools.name: qa.collect_artifacts
runner_tools.description: Normalize traces, screenshots, videos, network logs, and accessibility snapshots into report-ready artifact references.

runner_tools.name: qa.sync_campaign_results
runner_tools.description: Send sanitized campaign results to the web app under one campaign id.

competitive_scorecard.testspriteBaseline: URL, PRD, generated test plan, and black-box crawl are the public baseline.
competitive_scorecard.fusionAdvantage: Local MCP runner inspects repo structure, scripts, routes, API handlers, env expectations, and test tooling before planning.
competitive_scorecard.proofGate: Campaign cannot mark context complete until repo summary and runtime summary sync under one campaign ID.

competitive_scorecard.testspriteBaseline: Generic AI-generated happy-path and broad E2E coverage.
competitive_scorecard.fusionAdvantage: Each card includes risk, data needs, evidence gates, acceptance criteria, and blocked-gap policy.
competitive_scorecard.proofGate: Every generated card must include goal, steps, expected evidence, data needs, and acceptance criteria.

competitive_scorecard.testspriteBaseline: Reports failure and may suggest a fix.
competitive_scorecard.fusionAdvantage: Creates coding-agent repair packets with likely files, exact repro, expected behavior, verification command, and agent prompt.
competitive_scorecard.proofGate: Product bugs are not complete until a repair task can be handed to Codex, Claude, Cursor, or a local model.

competitive_scorecard.fusionAdvantage: Treats unsupported integrations and missing sandbox keys as blocked findings, never as implicit passes.
competitive_scorecard.proofGate: Audit score must include blocked checks and unsupported gaps as first-class report items.

competitive_scorecard.testspriteBaseline: Vendor model behavior is hidden behind the product.
competitive_scorecard.fusionAdvantage: Category router assigns repo context, runtime crawl, test generation, classification, repair writing, visual review, and traffic analysis to different managed, OpenRouter, Genspark, Docker, or proprietary model slots.
competitive_scorecard.proofGate: ModelTask contracts, provider slots, route assignments, fallbacks, and quality gates are exposed through the campaign API before live model invocation is enabled.

competitive_scorecard.fusionAdvantage: Vercel static/serverless app plus local runner keeps private code local while web reporting stays shareable.
competitive_scorecard.proofGate: Static app loads without framework dev server; API accepts runner sync; artifacts can move to Blob and campaign state to Postgres.

flagship_features.name: Traffic Insight Debugger
flagship_features.fusionVersion: Captured HTTP sessions become campaign evidence, finding context, and coding-agent repair packet inputs instead of separate debugging work.
flagship_features.whyItMatters: Coding agents and QA reports should reason from actual requests, responses, headers, status codes, and timing, not guesses from UI symptoms.
flagship_features.proofGate: Every failed API/network-related test must attach the exact request and response pair or mark traffic capture as blocked.

flagship_features.name: Audited Self-Healing
flagship_features.fusionVersion: Locator drift can be repaired, but every heal is logged as an audit event with visual/behavioral confirmation instead of silently turning green.
flagship_features.whyItMatters: Self-healing is useful only when it does not hide real product behavior changes or create false confidence.
flagship_features.proofGate: A healed test cannot be promoted unless the intent assertion and visual check still match the original test card.

flagship_features.name: AI Test Workspace
flagship_features.fusionVersion: Requirements, docs, code, tickets, existing Playwright scripts, and recorded user actions become one campaign knowledge base before tests are generated.
flagship_features.whyItMatters: The strongest test plans come from joined context, not a blank canvas or a single URL crawl.
flagship_features.proofGate: Every generated test card must show which source artifacts created it and what launch risk it covers.

heal_events.event: Report table action selector changed from data-testid=export-report to aria-label=Export audit report.
heal_events.auditNote: Selector can heal, but mobile visual overflow still fails, so the run remains red.

heal_events.event: Submit button text changed from Save to Submit.
heal_events.auditNote: Behavior assertion and network mutation stayed identical after heal.

model_provider_slots.label: Fusion managed default
model_provider_slots.guardrail: Used when no customer model route is configured; prompts and outputs are logged to the campaign audit trail.

model_provider_slots.label: OpenRouter coding model
model_provider_slots.guardrail: Allowed for code-aware work only after the project owner approves which repo excerpts can leave the local runner.

model_provider_slots.label: Genspark agent bridge
model_provider_slots.guardrail: Bridge receives sanitized tasks and artifact references, not raw production credentials.

model_provider_slots.label: Docker hosted local LLM
model_provider_slots.guardrail: Can run fully local for private code; must return structured JSON that passes the ModelTask schema.

model_provider_slots.label: Proprietary QA model
model_provider_slots.guardrail: Trained only from approved campaign artifacts; production secrets and credentials stay excluded from training data.

model_routes.label: Repo inspection summarizer
model_routes.qualityGate: Must cite files, scripts, routes, env keys, and unsupported gaps without inventing dependencies.

model_routes.label: Runtime workflow mapper
model_routes.qualityGate: Must produce route inventory, reachable states, console/network issues, and auth assumptions.

model_routes.label: Deep test-card generator
model_routes.qualityGate: Every card needs exact steps, data needs, evidence gates, risk, and acceptance criteria.

model_routes.label: Failure classifier
model_routes.qualityGate: Must classify as product bug, test bug, environment issue, flaky behavior, missing context, or unclear requirement.

model_routes.label: Coding-agent repair packet writer
model_routes.qualityGate: Must include likely files, repro steps, expected behavior, verification command, and a ready agent prompt.

model_routes.label: Visual and responsive auditor
model_routes.qualityGate: Must attach viewport, screenshot refs, overflow metrics, and concrete visual defects.

model_routes.label: Network traffic analyst
model_routes.qualityGate: Must map failed requests to cards/findings and redact sensitive headers before sync.

storage_readiness.label: Campaign database
storage_readiness.purpose: Durably stores projects, campaigns, test cards, runs, findings, repair tasks, model task logs, and artifact metadata.
storage_readiness.productionGate: A campaign must survive deploys and page refreshes without depending on seeded JSON.

storage_readiness.label: Evidence artifact store
storage_readiness.purpose: Stores screenshots, traces, videos, network logs, accessibility snapshots, and exported client reports.
storage_readiness.productionGate: Every failed or blocked test must have durable evidence refs that can be opened from the audit report.

storage_readiness.label: Runner sync authentication
storage_readiness.purpose: Protects local runner sync and artifact registration endpoints from unauthenticated writes.
storage_readiness.productionGate: Runner writes must be signed or rejected before accepting real customer campaign data.

database_tables.purpose: Launch audit campaign state and readiness score.
Local MCP runner identity, version, host, and last sync metadata.
Generated, reviewed, and executed evidence-gated test cards.
Per-test execution attempts with pass/fail/blocked state.
Blob-backed evidence metadata and redaction state.
Coding-agent-ready repair packets generated from product bugs.
Model routing audit trail, prompt/output hashes, latency, cost, and quality gate status.

storage_schema_sql:
create table if not exists projects (
  id text primary key,
  owner_id text not null,
  repo_url text,
  repo_path_hint text,
  framework text,
  support_tier text not null,
  created_at timestamptz not null default now()
);

create table if not exists campaigns (
  id text primary key,
  project_id text not null references projects(id),
  status text not null,
  app_url text not null,
  depth text not null,
  readiness_score integer not null default 0,
  created_at timestamptz not null default now(),
  updated_at timestamptz not null default now()
);

create table if not exists runner_sessions (
  id text primary key,
  campaign_id text not null references campaigns(id),
  runner_host text not null,
  version text not null,
  auth_state_ref text,
  last_sync_at timestamptz
);

create table if not exists test_cards (
  id text primary key,
  campaign_id text not null references campaigns(id),
  category text not null,
  risk text not null,
  status text not null,
  title text not null,
  goal text not null,
  steps jsonb not null,
  expected_evidence jsonb not null,
  data_needs jsonb not null,
  acceptance_criteria text not null
);

create table if not exists runs (
  id text primary key,
  campaign_id text not null references campaigns(id),
  test_card_id text not null references test_cards(id),
  status text not null,
  started_at timestamptz,
  ended_at timestamptz
);

create table if not exists artifacts (
  id text primary key,
  run_id text not null references runs(id),
  artifact_type text not null,
  blob_path text not null,
  sha256 text,
  redaction_status text not null default 'pending',
  created_at timestamptz not null default now()
);

create table if not exists findings (
  id text primary key,
  campaign_id text not null references campaigns(id),
  test_card_id text references test_cards(id),
  type text not null,
  severity text not null,
  title text not null,
  summary text not null,
  evidence_refs jsonb not null
);

create table if not exists repair_tasks (
  id text primary key,
  finding_id text not null references findings(id),
  severity text not null,
  title text not null,
  why_it_matters text not null,
  evidence_refs jsonb not null,
  likely_files jsonb not null,
  reproduction_steps jsonb not null,
  expected_behavior text not null,
  verification_command text not null,
  agent_prompt text not null
);

create table if not exists model_tasks (
  id text primary key,
  campaign_id text not null references campaigns(id),
  category text not null,
  provider_slot_id text not null,
  model text not null,
  status text not null,
  prompt_hash text,
  output_hash text,
  quality_gate text not null,
  latency_ms integer,
  cost_micros integer,
  created_at timestamptz not null default now()
);

alter table campaigns add column if not exists name text not null default 'Launch Audit Campaign';

alter table campaigns add column if not exists repo_path_hint text;

alter table test_cards add column if not exists exec jsonb not null default '[]'::jsonb;
